title: Iterative convergence in select_with_fee is correct; 2-pass is not
description: A contributor may suggest replacing the convergence loop with a 2-pass approach: estimate fee for 1 note, select notes, then recompute fee once for the actual count. 2-pass is wrong: if pass 2 selects more notes than pass 1, the fee computed from n1 inputs disagrees with FeeRule::standard() applied to n2 inputs, and builder.build rejects the transaction. The iterative loop is correct because (a) zip317_fee is monotone-non-decreasing with note count, (b) the note pool is finite, so the loop terminates in at most O(pool_size) iterations. In practice it converges in 1-2 iterations because Sapling fees step up only when inputs cross grace_actions boundaries.
